2605.29394.title: EvoMD-LLM: Learning the Language of Species Evolution in Reactive Molecular Dynamics
2605.29394.paper_summary: While large language models (LLMs) excel at static scientific reasoning, they struggle to model the temporal structure of dynamic physical processes. We present EvoMD-LLM (Evolutionary Molecular Dynamics Large Language Model), a framework that reformulates species-level molecular dynamics as a symbolic temporal language modeling problem. Reactive MD trajectories are discretized into sequences of molecular events, where each token represents a chemical species augmented with its persistence duration, enabling standard autoregressive LLMs to learn compositional evolution over time through efficient fine-tuning. A key component of EvoMD-LLM is temporal scaffolding, which treats event duration as an explicit linguistic token and serves as a structured inductive bias, significantly reducing invalid or hallucinated molecular outputs compared to conventional sequence modeling approaches. We evaluate EvoMD-LLM on multiple temporal prediction tasks, achieving up to 66.14% accuracy and consistently outperforming sequential neural networks and language-based baselines. Beyond quantitative improvements, we qualitatively observe that the model is capable of generating interpretations for its own predictions by incorporating relevant chemical knowledge, even though it was not explicitly supervised with paired trajectory-explanation data. These results demonstrate that symbolic temporal language modeling provides an effective framework for grounding LLMs in dynamic physical simulations.
2605.29394.paper_summary_zh: 雖然大型語言模型（LLMs）在靜態科學推理方面表現優異，但它們在建模動態物理過程的時間結構上卻面臨挑戰。<lb/>我們提出了EvoMD-LLM（進化分子動力學大型語言模型），這是一個將物種級別的分子動力學重新表述為符號時間語言建模問題的框架。<lb/>反應性MD軌跡被離散化為分子事件的序列，其中每個標記代表一種化學物質，並附加其持續時間，使得標準自回歸LLMs能夠通過高效的微調學習隨時間的組合演變。<lb/>EvoMD-LLM的一個關鍵組件是時間支架，它將事件持續時間視為一個明確的語言標記，並作為一種結構化的歸納偏差，顯著減少了與傳統序列建模方法相比的無效或幻覺的分子輸出。<lb/>我們在多個時間預測任務上評估EvoMD-LLM，達到高達66.14%的準確率，並始終超越序列神經網絡和基於語言的基準。<lb/>除了定量改進外，我們還質性地觀察到該模型能夠通過結合相關的化學知識生成對其自身預測的解釋，即使它並未明確地用配對的軌跡-解釋數據進行監督。<lb/>這些結果表明，符號時間語言建模為將LLMs根植於動態物理模擬提供了一個有效的框架。
2605.29394.author: Zhichen Tang et.al.
2605.29394.authors: Zhichen Tang, Zhengzheng Dang, Yulin Chen, Jixin Wu, Haiwen Li, Yanming Wang
